Class HistoryScreen

java.lang.Object
ui.BaseScreen
ui.HistoryScreen
public class HistoryScreen extends BaseScreen
Screen that displays the full expense history for the current cycle.

Features a sortable table, category filter toggle buttons, and inline edit/delete actions on selected rows.

  • Constructor Details

    • HistoryScreen

      public HistoryScreen(App app)
      Constructs the HistoryScreen.
      Parameters:
      app - the central application controller

  • Method Details

    • initComponents

      protected void initComponents()
      Initialises all UI components (fields, buttons, labels, etc.). Called before BaseScreen.initLayout().
      Specified by:
      initComponents in class BaseScreen

    • initLayout

      protected void initLayout()
      Arranges the initialised components into the BaseScreen.panel hierarchy. Called after BaseScreen.initComponents().
      Specified by:
      initLayout in class BaseScreen

    • initTransactionTableModel

      private DefaultTableModel initTransactionTableModel()
      Creates a non-editable table model with columns: Description, Category, Date, Amount.
      Returns:
      the configured DefaultTableModel

    • styleTransactionTable

      private JScrollPane styleTransactionTable(JTable table)
      Applies dark-themed cell renderers and styling to the transaction table, then wraps it in a scroll pane.
      Parameters:
      table - the table to style
      Returns:
      a styled JScrollPane containing the table

    • loadTransactions

      public void loadTransactions()
      Clears and reloads the table from the database. Also refreshes loadedExpenses used for row-level operations.

    • initFilters

      public void initFilters()
      Builds and registers all category filter toggle buttons. Clears any previously registered buttons before rebuilding.

    • applyFilter

      public void applyFilter()
      Applies the active category filter to the table row sorter. Shows all rows when "All" is selected; hides all rows when "None" is selected; otherwise filters to the selected category names.

    • clearFilter

      public void clearFilter()
      Resets all filters to the "All" state.

    • onEditClick

      public void onEditClick()
      Handles the "Edit" button click. Opens a dialog pre-filled with the selected expense's data and saves changes.

    • onDeleteClick

      public void onDeleteClick()
      Handles the "Delete" button click. Shows a confirmation dialog and deletes the selected expense on confirmation.

    • showEmptyState

      public void showEmptyState()
      Shows an information dialog prompting the user to select a row first.
